name: Blameless SRE Platform API
what_it_does: Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) platform with REST API for managing incidents, automated postmortems, SLO tracking, and reliability workflows — helping engineering teams build reliability culture with data-driven incident analysis.
use_cases: Creating and tracking incidents with rich SRE context via REST API
Querying SLO/SLI data for automated reliability reporting agents
Triggering postmortem creation after incident resolution via API
Syncing incident data with PagerDuty, Jira, and monitoring tools
Building reliability dashboards from Blameless SLO and incident data
not_for: Simple alerting and on-call rotation without SRE methodology focus
Teams not following SRE practices (blameless is SRE-methodology opinionated)
Small teams without dedicated reliability engineering function
best_when: A mature engineering team practicing SRE needs a dedicated platform for SLO management, automated postmortems, and reliability trend analysis with API integration.
avoid_when: You need basic incident management without SRE complexity, or are early-stage without reliability culture investment.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with service account tokens for API access. Bearer token authentication. Scoped access per resource type. Enterprise SSO integration via SAML/OIDC.

pricing.notes: Enterprise-focused pricing targeting larger engineering organizations. SRE platform specialist. Competes with Jeli, Rootly, FireHydrant. Strong focus on blameless incident culture and learning from failures.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers incidents, postmortems, SLOs, and services. OAuth2 with scopes for fine-grained access. Webhook support for incident lifecycle events. Enterprise documentation may require sales engagement.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incident and postmortem reads are idempotent. Creation operations generate new items. SLO data queries return consistent results. Update operations idempotent for same values.
known_agent_gotchas: Enterprise pricing — requires sales engagement before API access
SRE-methodology opinionated — concepts like SLOs must be configured before querying
OAuth2 service account setup required — more complex than simple API key
Postmortem templates must be configured in UI before API can trigger them

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 with scopes. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. Enterprise SSO via SAML/OIDC. Service account tokens for API access reduce credential exposure.
